```spthy
theory CERT_PATH_SIGNED_TOKEN
begin

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing

functions: h/1, cp/2

(*
  Certification-Path Signed Token Protocol (one-pass)
  - Identities are public keys.
  - cp(pkB, pkA) encodes a certification path "B → A" to let B recover pkA from the message.
  - A signs the hash of the message body.
  - B verifies recipient, signature, decrypts confidential data, and (abstractly) records replay parameters.
*)

let A(skA) =
  let pkA = pk(skA) in
  event RoleA(pkA);
  in(pkB);
  new tA;                      // timeliness tag
  new rA;                      // anti-replay nonce
  new sgnData;                 // non-confidential, integrity-protected data
  new encData;                 // confidential data for B only
  let C = aenc(encData, pkB) in
  let body = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, C> in
  let digest = h(body) in
  let sig = sign(digest, skA) in
  let token = <body, sig> in
  let certpath = cp(pkB, pkA) in
  event Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
  event Secret(encData, pkA, pkB);
  out(<certpath, token>)

let B(skB) =
  let pkB = pk(skB) in
  event RoleB(pkB);
  in(<cp(pkB, pkA), token>);                       // parse and recover pkA from cp(pkB, pkA)
  let <body, sig> = token in
  let <tA, rA, bIdent, sgnData, C> = body in
  if bIdent = pkB then
    let digest = h(body) in
    if verify(sig, digest, pkA) = true then
      let encData = adec(C, skB) in
      event Cache(pkB, tA, rA);                    // abstract replay cache record
      event Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData)

let LtkReveal(sk) =
  in(x);
  if x = pk(sk) then
    ( event Reveal(pk(sk)); out(sk) )


process:
(
  !(
    new skA; out(pk(skA)); event HonestA(pk(skA));
    ( LtkReveal(skA) | !A(skA) )
  )
|
  !(
    new skB; out(pk(skB)); event HonestB(pk(skB));
    ( LtkReveal(skB) | !B(skB) )
  )
)


(****************)
(*   Lemmas    *)
(****************)

(* 1) Secrecy of encData unless B is revealed *)
lemma secrecy_encData:
  "not(
     Ex encData pkA pkB #i #j #k.
       Secret(encData, pkA, pkB) @ i
       & HonestB(pkB) @ j
       & K(encData) @ k
       & not(Ex #r. Reveal(pkB) @ r)
   )"

(* 2) Injective agreement: B authenticates A with uniqueness (unless A is revealed) *)
lemma inj_agree_B:
  "All pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i.
     Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
     & (Ex #hA. HonestA(pkA) @ hA) & (Ex #hB. HonestB(pkB) @ hB)
   ==>
     ( Ex #j.
         Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j
         & j < i
         & not(Ex #i2.
                 Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i2
                 & not(#i2 = #i)
              )
     )
     | (Ex #r. Reveal(pkA) @ r)
  "

(* 3) Non-injective agreement: B authenticates A (unless A is revealed) *)
lemma noninj_agree_B:
  "All pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i.
     Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
     & (Ex #hA. HonestA(pkA) @ hA) & (Ex #hB. HonestB(pkB) @ hB)
   ==>
     (Ex #j. Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j & j < i)
     | (Ex #r. Reveal(pkA) @ r)
  "

(* 4) Executability / sanity *)
lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i #j #hA #hB.
      HonestA(pkA) @ hA
    & HonestB(pkB) @ hB
    & Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
    & Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j
    & i < j
    & not(Ex #r1. Reveal(pkA) @ r1)
    & not(Ex #r2. Reveal(pkB) @ r2)
  "

(* 5) Origin authentication of delivered confidential content *)
lemma content_agreement:
  "All pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i.
     Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
     & (Ex #hA. HonestA(pkA) @ hA) & (Ex #hB. HonestB(pkB) @ hB)
   ==>
     (Ex #j. Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j & j < i)
     | (Ex #r. Reveal(pkA) @ r)
  "

end
```

Notes: The certification path is modeled by the uninterpreted function cp(pkB, pkA). The replay cache is abstracted by the Cache(pkB, tA, rA) event; the injective-agreement lemma relies on freshness and intended replay protection.

theory CERT_PATH_SIGNED_TOKEN
begin

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing

functions: h/1, cp/2

(*
  Certification-Path Signed Token Protocol (one-pass)
  - Identities are public keys.
  - cp(pkB, pkA) encodes a certification path "B → A" to let B recover pkA from the message.
  - A signs the hash of the message body.
  - B verifies recipient, signature, decrypts confidential data, and (abstractly) records replay parameters.
*)

let A(skA) =
  let pkA = pk(skA) in
  event RoleA(pkA);
  in(pkB);
  new tA;                      // timeliness tag
  new rA;                      // anti-replay nonce
  new sgnData;                 // non-confidential, integrity-protected data
  new encData;                 // confidential data for B only
  let C = aenc(encData, pkB) in
  let body = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, C> in
  let digest = h(body) in
  let sig = sign(digest, skA) in
  let token = <body, sig> in
  let certpath = cp(pkB, pkA) in
  event Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
  event Secret(encData, pkA, pkB);
  out(<certpath, token>)

let B(skB) =
  let pkB = pk(skB) in
  event RoleB(pkB);
  in(<cp(pkB, pkA), token>);                       // parse and recover pkA from cp(pkB, pkA)
  let <body, sig> = token in

    let digest = h(body) in
    if verify(sig, digest, pkA) = true then
      let encData = adec(C, skB) in
      event Cache(pkB, tA, rA);                    // abstract replay cache record
      event Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData)

let LtkReveal(sk) =
  in(x);
  if x = pk(sk) then
    ( event Reveal(pk(sk)); out(sk) )


process:
(
  !(
    new skA; out(pk(skA)); event HonestA(pk(skA));
    ( LtkReveal(skA) | !A(skA) )
  )
|
  !(
    new skB; out(pk(skB)); event HonestB(pk(skB));
    ( LtkReveal(skB) | !B(skB) )
  )
)

(*   Lemmas    *)
(****************)

(* 1) Secrecy of encData unless B is revealed *)
lemma secrecy_encData:
  "not(
     Ex encData pkA pkB #i #j #k.
       Secret(encData, pkA, pkB) @ i
       & HonestB(pkB) @ j
       & K(encData) @ k

   )"

(* 2) Injective agreement: B authenticates A with uniqueness (unless A is revealed) *)
lemma inj_agree_B:
  "All pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i.
     Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
     & (Ex #hA. HonestA(pkA) @ hA) & (Ex #hB. HonestB(pkB) @ hB)
   ==>
     ( Ex #j.
         Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j
         & j < i
         & not(Ex #i2.
                 Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i2
                 & not(#i2 = #i)

(* 3) Non-injective agreement: B authenticates A (unless A is revealed) *)

       & HonestB(pkB) @ j
       & K(encData) @ k
       & not(Ex #r. Reveal(pkB) @ r)
   )"

(* 2) Injective agreement: B authenticates A with uniqueness (unless A is revealed) *)
lemma inj_agree_B:
  "All pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i.
     Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
     & (Ex #hA. HonestA(pkA) @ hA) & (Ex #hB. HonestB(pkB) @ hB)
   ==>
     ( Ex #j.
         Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j
         & j < i
         & not(Ex #i2.
                 Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i2
                 & not(#i2 = #i)
              )
     )
     | (Ex #r. Reveal(pkA) @ r)
  "

(* 3) Non-injective agreement: B authenticates A (unless A is revealed) *)
lemma noninj_agree_B:
  "All pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i.
     Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
     & (Ex #hA. HonestA(pkA) @ hA) & (Ex #hB. HonestB(pkB) @ hB)
   ==>
     (Ex #j. Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j & j < i)
     | (Ex #r. Reveal(pkA) @ r)
  "

(* 4) Executability / sanity *)
lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i #j #hA #hB.
      HonestA(pkA) @ hA
    & HonestB(pkB) @ hB
    & Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
    & Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j
    & i < j
    & not(Ex #r1. Reveal(pkA) @ r1)
    & not(Ex #r2. Reveal(pkB) @ r2)
  "

(* 5) Origin authentication of delivered confidential content *)
lemma content_agreement:
  "All pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i.
     Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
     & (Ex #hA. HonestA(pkA) @ hA) & (Ex #hB. HonestB(pkB) @ hB)
   ==>
     (Ex #j. Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j & j < i)
     | (Ex #r. Reveal(pkA) @ r)
  "

end

theory CERT_PATH_SIGNED_TOKEN
begin

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing

functions: h/1, cp/2

/* 
  Certification-Path Signed Token Protocol (one-pass)
  - Identities are public keys.
  - cp(pkB, pkA) encodes a certification path "B → A" to let B recover pkA from the message.
  - A signs the hash of the message body.
  - B verifies recipient, signature, decrypts confidential data, and (abstractly) records replay parameters.
*/

let A(skA) =
  let pkA = pk(skA) in
  event RoleA(pkA);
  in(pkB);
  new tA;                      // timeliness tag
  new rA;                      // anti-replay nonce
  new sgnData;                 // non-confidential, integrity-protected data
  new encData;                 // confidential data for B only
  let C = aenc(encData, pkB) in
  let body = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, C> in
  let digest = h(body) in
  let sig = sign(digest, skA) in
  let token = <body, sig> in
  let certpath = cp(pkB, pkA) in
  event Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
  event Secret(encData, pkA, pkB);
  out(<certpath, token>)

let B(skB) =
  let pkB = pk(skB) in
  event RoleB(pkB);
  in(<cp(pkB, pkA), token>);                       // parse and recover pkA from cp(pkB, pkA)
  let <body, sig> = token in
  let <tA, rA, bIdent, sgnData, C> = body in
  if bIdent = pkB then
    let digest = h(body) in
    if verify(sig, digest, pkA) = true then
      let encData = adec(C, skB) in
      event Cache(pkB, tA, rA);                    // abstract replay cache record
      event Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData)

let LtkReveal(sk) =
  in(x);
  if x = pk(sk) then
    ( event Reveal(pk(sk)); out(sk) )


process:
  (
    !(
      new skA; out(pk(skA)); event HonestA(pk(skA));
      ( LtkReveal(skA) | !A(skA) )
    )
  |
    !(
      new skB; out(pk(skB)); event HonestB(pk(skB));
      ( LtkReveal(skB) | !B(skB) )
    )
  )


/****************
 *   Lemmas    *
 ****************/

(* 1) Secrecy of encData unless B is revealed *)
lemma secrecy_encData:
  "not(
     Ex encData pkA pkB #i #j #k.
       Secret(encData, pkA, pkB) @ i
       & HonestB(pkB) @ j
       & K(encData) @ k
       & not(Ex #r. Reveal(pkB) @ r)
   )"

(* 2) Injective agreement: B authenticates A with uniqueness (unless A is revealed) *)
lemma inj_agree_B:
  "All pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i.
     Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
     & (Ex #hA. HonestA(pkA) @ hA) & (Ex #hB. HonestB(pkB) @ hB)
   ==>
     ( Ex #j.
         Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j
         & j < i
         & not(Ex #i2.
                 Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i2
                 & not(#i2 = #i)
              )
     )
     | (Ex #r. Reveal(pkA) @ r)
  "

(* 3) Non-injective agreement: B authenticates A (unless A is revealed) *)
lemma noninj_agree_B:
  "All pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i.
     Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
     & (Ex #hA. HonestA(pkA) @ hA) & (Ex #hB. HonestB(pkB) @ hB)
   ==>
     (Ex #j. Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j & j < i)
     | (Ex #r. Reveal(pkA) @ r)
  "

(* 4) Executability / sanity *)
lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i #j #hA #hB.
      HonestA(pkA) @ hA
    & HonestB(pkB) @ hB
    & Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
    & Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j
    & i < j
    & not(Ex #r1. Reveal(pkA) @ r1)
    & not(Ex #r2. Reveal(pkB) @ r2)
  "

(* 5) Origin authentication of delivered confidential content *)
lemma content_agreement:
  "All pkA pkB tA rA sgnData encData #i.
     Commit('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ i
     & (Ex #hA. HonestA(pkA) @ hA) & (Ex #hB. HonestB(pkB) @ hB)
   ==>
     (Ex #j. Running('A','B', pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) @ j & j < i)
     | (Ex #r. Reveal(pkA) @ r)
  "

end

generated_prompt:
# SAPIC+ Security-Protocol Prompt Framework

Fill in every `⟨…⟩` placeholder and follow every `[Instruction: …]` note to instantiate this framework for a specific security protocol. The completed text is a prompt to be given to an LLM, which should then produce a full Tamarin Prover model of the protocol written in the SAPIC+ process calculus.

---

## 1. Summary

Write a formal security-protocol model in Tamarin Prover using the SAPIC+ process calculus (applied-pi style let/process blocks, not multiset rewrite rules). The protocol to model is Certification-Path Signed Token Protocol (Section 7.5), in which an Initiator A sends a single signed-and-encrypted one-pass message to a Responder B, aiming to provide B with origin authentication, integrity, and non-repudiation of non-confidential parameters and confidentiality of confidential parameters.

## 2. Cryptographic setup

- Use the built-in equational theories for:
  - asymmetric (public-key) encryption (aenc/adec, pk),
  - digital signatures (sign/verify),
  - pairing/tupling.
- Declare additional user-defined function symbols:
  - h/1 — a one-argument hash function used in signatures (no equations beyond standard hashing-as-data),
  - cp/2 — an uninterpreted function symbol encoding the sent certification path “B → A” that allows B to obtain A’s public key from the message payload (no equations).
- Global assumptions and conventions:
  - Identities are modeled as public keys (pkA, pkB).
  - A’s signature is over the hash of the entire message body: verify(sign(h(body), skA), pk(skA)) = h(body) holds via the built-in signature theory.
  - The certification path “B → A” is modeled as a public token cp(pkB, pkA) sent alongside the signed token; PKI validation is abstracted to pattern-matching cp(pkB, pkA) to recover pkA.
  - Replay handling: A generates a fresh rA per run. B maintains a replay cache keyed by (pkB, tA, rA) and rejects repeats; this models the described optional replay check using a sequential part and storage.

## 3. Roles

- Role name and informal identity — parameters:
  - Initiator A — parameters: skA (A’s long-term signing key), pkA (= pk(skA)) known to others, and the peer’s public key pkB obtained at run-time from the network.
  - Responder B — parameters: skB (B’s long-term decryption key), pkB (= pk(skB)).

- Initiator A (A):
  1. Role-claim event:
     - Emit event RoleA(pkA).
  2. Fresh-value generation:
     - tA: fresh timestamp/validity tag to indicate timeliness.
     - rA: fresh non-repeating number (nonce) used to detect replay/forgery.
     - sgnData: fresh non-confidential parameters to be integrity-protected and non-repudiable (modeled as fresh but not treated as secret).
     - encData: fresh confidential parameters (e.g., secret keys) to be kept secret from the attacker and any party other than B.
  3. Message construction:
     - Compute C = aenc(encData, pkB).
     - Construct body = (tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, C).
     - Compute sig = sign(h(body), skA).
     - Construct token = (body, sig).
     - Construct certpath = cp(pkB, pkA) to accompany the token.
     - Outgoing message is M1 = (certpath, token).
  4. Message reception and checks:
     - None (one-pass protocol).
  5. Security-relevant events:
     - Emit Running(A, B, pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) immediately before sending M1, capturing A’s view of the intended peer and data.
     - Emit Secret(encData, pkA, pkB) to mark encData as intended to be confidential between A and B.
  6. Send/receive actions:
     - Send M1 over the network.

- Responder B (B):
  1. Role-claim event:
     - Emit event RoleB(pkB).
  2. Fresh-value generation:
     - None (B does not generate fresh data for this one-pass receive).
  3. Message reception and checks:
     - Receive M1 = (certpath, token).
     - Parse certpath as cp(pkB, pkA) to obtain pkA (abstracting the certification path “B → A”).
     - Parse token as (body, sig).
     - Parse body as (tA, rA, bIdent, sgnData, C).
     - Check recipient identity: require bIdent = pkB. Abort otherwise.
     - Verify signature: require verify(sig, pkA) = h(body). Abort otherwise.
     - Decrypt C with skB to obtain encData = adec(C, skB).
     - Timeliness/replay handling:
       - Check that (pkB, tA, rA) has not been seen before in a replay cache; abort on repeat.
       - Insert (pkB, tA, rA) into the replay cache to prevent future acceptance of the same token during the validity window.
  4. Security-relevant events:
     - After all checks succeed and decryption completes, emit Commit(A, B, pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData).
  5. Send/receive actions:
     - Receive M1 over the network.
     - No response message is sent (one-pass protocol).

- Key-compromise helper:
  - For any long-term asymmetric key pair (sk, pk), allow compromise on attacker request:
    - Upon receiving pk from the network, emit Reveal(pk) and output sk to the network.
  - One such helper is spawned per generated long-term key (for A’s signing key and for B’s decryption key).

## 4. Top-level process

- Composition and replication:
  - In parallel, create unboundedly many A identities:
    - For each instance:
      - Generate fresh skA and compute pkA = pk(skA).
      - Publish pkA to the network.
      - Emit HonestA(pkA).
      - In parallel:
        - Spawn the key-compromise helper for (skA, pkA).
        - Allow unboundedly many protocol runs of role A instantiated with (skA, pkA); in each run, A obtains a peer pkB from the network before constructing and sending M1 as specified.
  - In parallel, create unboundedly many B identities:
    - For each instance:
      - Generate fresh skB and compute pkB = pk(skB).
      - Publish pkB to the network.
      - Emit HonestB(pkB).
      - In parallel:
        - Spawn the key-compromise helper for (skB, pkB).
        - Allow unboundedly many protocol runs of role B instantiated with (skB, pkB); each run waits to receive M1 and processes it as specified, maintaining a per-B replay cache keyed by (pkB, tA, rA).

## 5. Lemmas to include

1. Secrecy — encData confidentiality:
   - For all encData, pkA, pkB: if Secret(encData, pkA, pkB) occurs and HonestB(pkB) holds, then the attacker never learns encData (¬K(encData)) unless B’s long-term key is compromised, i.e., Reveal(pkB) occurs.
   - Rationale: encData is only ever sent encrypted under pkB; disclosure is allowed only if B is revealed.

2. Injective agreement (B authenticates A):
   - For all pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData:
     - If Commit(A, B, pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) occurs and HonestA(pkA) ∧ HonestB(pkB) ∧ ¬Reveal(pkA), then there exists an earlier Running(A, B, pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData).
     - Injectivity: moreover, there is no second distinct Commit(A, B, pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) at a different time in the trace under the same honesty and non-reveal assumptions.
   - Rationale: B’s acceptance implies a unique prior A run on the same data; injectivity relies on B’s replay cache and A’s fresh rA.

3. Non-injective agreement (B authenticates A):
   - Same as above but without the injectivity condition: every Commit(A, B, ...) by B corresponds to at least one earlier Running(A, B, ...) by A on the same tuple, unless Reveal(pkA).

4. Sanity / executability:
   - There exists a trace with HonestA(pkA) and HonestB(pkB) and with no Reveal(pkA) and no Reveal(pkB), in which A emits Running(A, B, pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) and B emits a matching Commit(A, B, pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData).

5. Optional protocol-specific lemma (origin authentication of delivered confidential content):
   - If Commit(A, B, pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) occurs under HonestA(pkA) ∧ HonestB(pkB) ∧ ¬Reveal(pkA), then the encData value B accepted is exactly the one A intended in its Running(A, B, pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) event (agreement on confidential content), as witnessed by the matching tuples.

## 6. Conclusive sentence

Name the theory something like CERT_PATH_SIGNED_TOKEN; use consistent role tags A and B in all agreement tuples, and keep event names, arities, and argument orders identical between the process definitions and the lemmas.
